verses.latin_vulgate: Ego vir videns paupertatem meam  in virga indignationis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֤י הַגֶּ֙בֶר֙ רָאָ֣ה עֳנִ֔י בְּשֵׁ֖בֶט עֶבְרָתֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Aleph. I am the man that see my poverty by the rod of his indignation.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen mies, jonka viheliäisyyttä nähdä täytyy hänen hirmuisuutensa vitsan kautta.
verses.king_james: I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Me minavit, et adduxit in tenebras,  et non in lucem.
verses.douay_rheims: Aleph. He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, and not into light.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän johdatti minua ja vei pimeyteen ja ei valkeuteen.
verses.king_james: He hath led me, and brought me into darkness, but not into light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tantum in me vertit et convertit  manum suam tota die.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֣ךְ בִּ֥י יָשֻׁ֛ב יַהֲפֹ֥ךְ יָד֖וֹ כׇּל־הַיּֽוֹם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Aleph. Only against me he hath turned, and turned again his hand all the day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on kätensä kääntänyt minua vastaan, ja toimittaa toisin aina minun kanssani.
verses.king_james: Surely against me is he turned; he turneth his hand against me all the day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vetustam fecit pellem meam et carnem meam ;  contrivit ossa mea.
verses.douay_rheims: Beth. My skin and my flesh he hath made old, he hath broken my bones.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on tehnyt lihani ja nahkani vanhaksi, ja luuni musertanut.
verses.king_james: My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ædificavit in gyro meo, et circumdedit me  felle et labore.
verses.douay_rheims: Beth. He hath built round about me, and he hath compassed me with gall, and labour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän rakensi minua vastaan, ja sapella ja vaivalla hän minua kääri.
verses.king_james: He hath builded against me, and compassed me with gall and travail.

verses.latin_vulgate: In tenebrosis collocavit me,  quasi mortuos sempiternos.
verses.douay_rheims: Beth. He hath set me in dark places as those that are dead for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on minut pannut pimeyteen, niinkuin aikaa kuolleet.
verses.king_james: He hath set me in dark places, as they that be dead of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumædificavit adversum me, ut non egrediar ;  aggravavit compedem meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גָּדַ֧ר בַּעֲדִ֛י וְלֹ֥א אֵצֵ֖א הִכְבִּ֥יד נְחׇשְׁתִּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ghimel. He hath built against me round about, that I may not get out: he hath made my fetters heavy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on minut muurannut sisälle, etten minä pääse ulos, ja minut kovaan jalkapuuhun pannut.
verses.king_james: He hath hedged me about, that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et cum clamavero, et rogavero,  exclusit orationem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם כִּ֤י אֶזְעַק֙ וַאֲשַׁוֵּ֔עַ שָׂתַ֖ם תְּפִלָּתִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ghimel. Yea, and when I cry, and entreat, he hath shut out my prayer.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaikka minä huudan ja parun, niin hän kuitenkin korvansa tukitsee minun rukouksestani.
verses.king_james: Also when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conclusit vias meas lapidibus quadris ;  semitas meas subvertit.
verses.douay_rheims: Ghimel. He hath shut up my ways with square stones, he hath turned my paths upside down.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on muurannut tieni kiinni vuojonkivillä, ja polkuni sulkenut.
verses.king_james: He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ursus insidians factus est mihi,  leo in absconditis.
verses.douay_rheims: Daleth. He is become to me as a bear lying in wait: as a lion in secret places.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on väijynyt minua niinkuin karhu, niinkuin jalopeura salaisuudessa.
verses.king_james: He was unto me as a bear lying in wait, and as a lion in secret places.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semitas meas subvertit, et confregit me ;  posuit me desolatam.
verses.douay_rheims: Daleth. He hath turned aside my paths, and hath broken me in pieces, he hath made me desolate.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on minun antanut tieltä eksyä, ja minut repinyt säpäleiksi, ja minut autioksi tehnyt.
verses.king_james: He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces: he hath made me desolate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tetendit arcum suum, et posuit me  quasi signum ad sagittam.
verses.douay_rheims: Daleth. He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for his arrows.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on joutsensa jännittänyt, ja asettanut minun nuolella tarkoitettavaksi.
verses.king_james: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit in renibus meis  filias pharetræ suæ.
verses.douay_rheims: He. He hath shot into my reins the daughters of his quiver.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän ampui minun munaskuihini viinensä nuolet.
verses.king_james: He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factus sum in derisum omni populo meo,  canticum eorum tota die.
verses.douay_rheims: He. I am made a derision to all my people, their song all the day long.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen kaiken minun kansani nauru, ja heidän jokapäiväinen virtensä.
verses.king_james: I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Replevit me amaritudinibus ;  inebriavit me absinthio.
verses.douay_rheims: He. He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath inebriated me with wormwood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on haikeudella minut ravinnut, ja koiruoholla juovuttanut.
verses.king_james: He hath filled me with bitterness, he hath made me drunken with wormwood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fregit ad numerum dentes meos ;  cibavit me cinere.
verses.douay_rheims: Vau. And he hath broken my teeth one by one, he hath fed me with ashes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän on hampaani somerolla rikki musertanut, hän kieritti minun tuhassa.
verses.king_james: He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et repulsa est a pace anima mea ;  oblitus sum bonorum.
verses.douay_rheims: Vau. And my soul is removed far off from peace, I have forgotten good things.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun sieluni on ajettu pois rauhasta, minun täytyy hyvän unohtaa.
verses.king_james: And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I forgat prosperity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi : Periit finis meus,  et spes mea a Domino.
verses.douay_rheims: Vau. And I said: My end and my hope is perished from the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä sanoin: minun voimani ja minun toivoni Herran päälle on kadonnut.
verses.king_james: And I said, My strength and my hope is perished from the Lord:

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordare paupertatis, et transgressionis meæ,  absinthii et fellis.
verses.douay_rheims: Zain. Remember my poverty, and transgression, the wormwood and the gall.
verses.finnish_biblia: Muista siis, kuinka minä niin raadollinen ja hyljätty, koiruoholla ja sapella juotettu olen.
verses.king_james: Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Memoria memor ero, et tabescet  in me anima mea.
verses.douay_rheims: Zain. I will be mindful and remember, and my soul shall languish within me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun sieluni sen kyllä muistaa, ja sitä itsellensä tutkistelee.
verses.king_james: My soul hath them still in remembrance, and is humbled in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc recolens in corde meo,  ideo sperabo.
verses.douay_rheims: Zain. These things I shall think over in my heart, therefore will I hope.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä panen sen sydämeeni; sentähden minä vielä nyt toivon.
verses.king_james: This I recall to my mind, therefore have I hope.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misericordiæ Domini, quia non sumus consumpti ;  quia non defecerunt miserationes ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַֽסְדֵ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ כִּ֣י לֹא־תָ֔מְנוּ כִּ֥י לֹא־כָל֖וּ רַחֲמָֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Heth. The mercies of the Lord that we are not consumed: because his commiserations have not failed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herran laupiudesta se on, ettemme ratki hukkuneet; ei hänen laupiutensa vielä loppunut.
verses.king_james: It is of the Lord’s mercies that we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Novi diluculo,  multa est fides tua.
verses.douay_rheims: Heth. They are new every morning, great is thy faithfulness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan joka huomen se on uusi, ja sinun uskollisuutes on suuri.
verses.king_james: They are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pars mea Dominus, dixit anima mea ;  propterea exspectabo eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֶלְקִ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ אָמְרָ֣ה נַפְשִׁ֔י עַל־כֵּ֖ן אוֹחִ֥יל לֽוֹ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Heth. The Lord is my portion, said my soul: therefore will I wait for him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra on minun osani, sanoo minun sieluni; sentähden tahdon minä häneen toivoa.
verses.king_james: The Lord is my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonus est Dominus sperantibus in eum,  animæ quærenti illum.
verses.douay_rheims: Teth. The Lord is good to them that hope in him, to the soul that seeketh him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra on hyvä niille, jotka häneen toivovat, ja niille sieluille, jotka häntä kysyvät.
verses.king_james: The Lord is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul that seeketh him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum est præstolari cum silentio  salutare Dei.
verses.douay_rheims: Teth. It is good to wait with silence for the salvation of God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hyvä on olla kärsivällisenä ja Herralta apua toivoa.
verses.king_james: It is good that a man should both hope and quietly wait for the salvation of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum est viro cum portaverit jugum  ab adolescentia sua.
verses.douay_rheims: Teth. It is good for a man, when he hath borne the yoke from his youth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hyvä on ihmiselle ijestä kantaa nuoruudessansa;
verses.king_james: It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sedebit solitarius, et tacebit,  quia levavit super se.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵשֵׁ֤ב בָּדָד֙ וְיִדֹּ֔ם כִּ֥י נָטַ֖ל עָלָֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Jod. He shall sit solitary, and hold his peace: because he hath taken it up upon himself.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että hän istuu yksinänsä, on vaiti, kuin jotakin hänen päällensä tulee,
verses.king_james: He sitteth alone and keepeth silence, because he hath borne it upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ponet in pulvere os suum,  si forte sit spes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִתֵּ֤ן בֶּֽעָפָר֙ פִּ֔יהוּ אוּלַ֖י יֵ֥שׁ תִּקְוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Jod. He shall put his mouth in the dust, if so be there may be hope.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja panee suunsa tomuun, ja odottaa toivoa,
verses.king_james: He putteth his mouth in the dust; if so be there may be hope.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dabit percutienti se maxillam :  saturabitur opprobriis.
verses.douay_rheims: Jod. He shall give his cheek to him that striketh him, he shall be filled with reproaches.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja antaa löydä poskillensa, ja paljon pilkkaa kärsii.
verses.king_james: He giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him: he is filled full with reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia non repellet  in sempiternum Dominus.
verses.douay_rheims: Caph. For the Lord will not cast off for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ei Herra syökse pois ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: For the Lord will not cast off for ever:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia si abjecit, et miserebitur,  secundum multitudinem misericordiarum suarum.
verses.douay_rheims: Caph. For if he hath cast off, he will also have mercy, according to the multitude of his mercies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan hän saattaa murheelliseksi, ja taas armahtaa suuresta laupiudestansa.
verses.king_james: But though he cause grief, yet will he have compassion according to the multitude of his mercies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim humiliavit ex corde suo  et abjecit filios hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י לֹ֤א עִנָּה֙ מִלִּבּ֔וֹ וַיַּגֶּ֖ה בְּנֵי־אִֽישׁ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Caph. For he hath not willingly afflicted, nor cast off the children of men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä ei hän sydämestänsä ihmisiä kurita eikä murheesen saata,
verses.king_james: For he doth not afflict willingly nor grieve the children of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut conteret sub pedibus suis  omnes vinctos terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְדַכֵּא֙ תַּ֣חַת רַגְלָ֔יו כֹּ֖ל אֲסִ֥ירֵי אָֽרֶץ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Lamed. To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the land,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin hän tahtois raadolliset maan päällä ratki jalkainsa alla polkea,
verses.king_james: To crush under his feet all the prisoners of the earth,

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut declinaret judicium viri  in conspectu vultus Altissimi.
verses.douay_rheims: Lamed. To turn aside the judgment of a man before the face of the most High,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja antais jonkun miehen oikeuden Ylimmäisen edessä tulla käännetyksi pois,
verses.king_james: To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the most High,

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut perverteret hominem in judicio suo ;  Dominus ignoravit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְעַוֵּ֤ת אָדָם֙ בְּרִיב֔וֹ אֲדֹנָ֖י לֹ֥א רָאָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Lamed. To destroy a man wrongfully in his judgment, the Lord hath not approved.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ihmistä väärin tuomita asiassansa, niinkuin ei Herra sitä näkisikään.
verses.king_james: To subvert a man in his cause, the Lord approveth not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis est iste qui dixit ut fieret,  Domino non jubente ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֣י זֶ֤ה אָמַר֙ וַתֶּ֔הִי אֲדֹנָ֖י לֹ֥א צִוָּֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Mem. Who is he that hath commanded a thing to be done, when the Lord commandeth it not?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka tohtii siis sanoa: senkaltaiset tapahtuvat ilman Herran käskyä?
verses.king_james: Who is he that saith, and it cometh to pass, when the Lord commandeth it not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex ore Altissimi non egredientur  nec mala nec bona ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִפִּ֤י עֶלְיוֹן֙ לֹ֣א תֵצֵ֔א הָרָע֖וֹת וְהַטּֽוֹב׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Mem. Shall not both evil and good proceed out of the mouth of the Highest?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö paha ja hyvä tule Korkeimman suusta?
verses.king_james: Out of the mouth of the most High proceedeth not evil and good?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid murmuravit homo vivens,  vir pro peccatis suis ?
verses.douay_rheims: Mem. Why hath a living man murmured, man suffering for his sins?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi siis ihmiset nurisevat eläissänsä? Jokainen nuriskaan syntejänsä vastaan.
verses.king_james: Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?

verses.latin_vulgate: Scrutemur vias nostras, et quæramus,  et revertamur ad Dominum.
verses.douay_rheims: Nun. Let us search our ways, and seek, and return to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tutkistelkaamme ja etsikäämme meidän menoamme, ja palatkaamme Herran tykö.
verses.king_james: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levemus corda nostra cum manibus  ad Dominum in cælos.
verses.douay_rheims: Nun. Let us lift up our hearts with our hands to the Lord in the heavens.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nostakaamme meidän sydämemme ja kätemme taivaasen päin, Jumalan tykö.
verses.king_james: Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos inique egimus, et ad iracundiam provocavimus ;  idcirco tu inexorabilis es.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֤חְנוּ פָשַׁ֙עְנוּ֙ וּמָרִ֔ינוּ אַתָּ֖ה לֹ֥א סָלָֽחְתָּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Nun. We have done wickedly, and provoked thee to wrath: therefore thou art inexorable.
verses.finnish_biblia: Me, me olemme syntiä tehneet ja kovakorvaiset olleet; (sentähden) et sinä säästänytkään.
verses.king_james: We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Operuisti in furore, et percussisti nos ;  occidisti, nec pepercisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סַכּ֤וֹתָה בָאַף֙ וַֽתִּרְדְּפֵ֔נוּ הָרַ֖גְתָּ לֹ֥א חָמָֽלְתָּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Samech. Thou hast covered in thy wrath, and hast struck us: thou hast killed and hast not spared.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan sinä olet vihalla meitä peittänyt ja vainonnut, ja armottomasti surmannut.
verses.king_james: Thou hast covered with anger, and persecuted us: thou hast slain, thou hast not pitied.

verses.latin_vulgate: Opposuisti nubem tibi,  ne transeat oratio.
verses.douay_rheims: Samech. Thou hast set a cloud before thee, that our prayer may not pass through.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä verhoitit itses pilvellä, ettei rukous päässyt sen lävitse.
verses.king_james: Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that our prayer should not pass through.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eradicationem et abjectionem posuisti me  in medio populorum.
verses.douay_rheims: Samech. Thou hast made me as an outcast, and refuse in the midst of the people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä olet meitä tunkioksi ja saastaisuudeksi kansain seassa tehnyt.
verses.king_james: Thou hast made us as the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperuerunt super nos os suum  omnes inimici.
verses.douay_rheims: Phe. All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki meidän vihollisemme ovat suutansa ammottaneet meitä vastaan.
verses.king_james: All our enemies have opened their mouths against us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Formido et laqueus facta est nobis  vaticinatio, et contritio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פַּ֧חַד וָפַ֛חַת הָ֥יָה לָ֖נוּ הַשֵּׁ֥את וְהַשָּֽׁבֶר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Phe. Prophecy is become to us a fear, and a snare, and destruction.
verses.finnish_biblia: Me painetaan alas ja rangaistaan pelvolla ja ahdistuksella.
verses.king_james: Fear and a snare is come upon us, desolation and destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divisiones aquarum deduxit oculus meus,  in contritione filiæ populi mei.
verses.douay_rheims: Phe. My eye hath run down with streams of water, for the destruction of the daughter of my people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun silmäni vuotavat vesi-ojia minun kansani tyttären surkeuden tähden.
verses.king_james: Mine eye runneth down with rivers of water for the destruction of the daughter of my people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculus meus afflictus est, nec tacuit,  eo quod non esset requies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינִ֧י נִגְּרָ֛ה וְלֹ֥א תִדְמֶ֖ה מֵאֵ֥ין הֲפֻגֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ain. My eye is afflicted, and hath not been quiet, because there was no rest:
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun silmäni vuotavat ja ei taida lakata; ei he asetu,
verses.king_james: Mine eye trickleth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission,

verses.latin_vulgate: Donec respiceret et videret  Dominus de cælis.
verses.douay_rheims: Ain. Till the Lord regarded and looked down from the heavens.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siihenasti että Herra katsoo taivaasta alas ja näkee.
verses.king_james: Till the Lord look down, and behold from heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculus meus deprædatus est animam meam  in cunctis filiabus urbis meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינִי֙ עֽוֹלְלָ֣ה לְנַפְשִׁ֔י מִכֹּ֖ל בְּנ֥וֹת עִירִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Ain. My eye hath wasted my soul because of all the daughters of my city.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun silmäni kuluttaa minulta elämäni kaikkein minun kaupunkini tytärten tähden.
verses.king_james: Mine eye affecteth mine heart because of all the daughters of my city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venatione ceperunt me quasi avem  inimici mei gratis.
verses.douay_rheims: Sade. My enemies have chased me and caught me like a bird, without cause.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun viholliseni ovat minua kovin ajaneet takaa, niinkuin lintua ilman syytä.
verses.king_james: Mine enemies chased me sore, like a bird, without cause.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lapsa est in lacum vita mea,  et posuerunt lapidem super me.
verses.douay_rheims: Sade. My life is fallen into the pit, and they have laid a stone over me.
verses.finnish_biblia: He ovat minun elämäni kuoppaan salvanneet, ja heittäneet kiven minun päälleni.
verses.king_james: They have cut off my life in the dungeon, and cast a stone upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inundaverunt aquæ super caput meum ;  dixi : Perii.
verses.douay_rheims: Sade. Waters have flowed over my head: I said: I am cut off.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ovat myös vedet minun pääni ylitse käyneet; niin minä sanoin: nyt minä ratki hukassa olen.
verses.king_james: Waters flowed over mine head; then I said, I am cut off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invocavi nomen tuum, Domine,  de lacu novissimo.
verses.douay_rheims: Coph. I have called upon thy name, O Lord, from the lowest pit.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä huusin avukseni sinun nimeäs, Herra, alhaalta kuopasta;
verses.king_james: I called upon thy name, O Lord, out of the low dungeon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocem meam audisti ; ne avertas aurem tuam  a singultu meo et clamoribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוֹלִ֖י שָׁמָ֑עְתָּ אַל־תַּעְלֵ֧ם אׇזְנְךָ֛ לְרַוְחָתִ֖י לְשַׁוְעָתִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Coph. Thou hast heard my voice: turn not away thy ear from my sighs, and cries.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinä kuulit minun ääneni, älä korvias kätke minun huokauksestani ja huudostani.
verses.king_james: Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Appropinquasti in die quando invocavi te ;  dixisti : Ne timeas.
verses.douay_rheims: Coph. Thou drewest near in the day, when I called upon thee, thou saidst: Fear not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä lähenet kuin minä sinua huudan, ja sanot: älä pelkää.
verses.king_james: Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee: thou saidst, Fear not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judicasti, Domine, causam animæ meæ,  redemptor vitæ meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֧בְתָּ אֲדֹנָ֛י רִיבֵ֥י נַפְשִׁ֖י גָּאַ֥לְתָּ חַיָּֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Res. Thou hast judged, O Lord, the cause of my soul, thou the Redeemer of my life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä, Herra, ratkaiset minun sieluni asian, ja lunastat minun henkeni.
verses.king_james: O Lord, thou hast pleaded the causes of my soul; thou hast redeemed my life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidisti, Domine, iniquitatem illorum adversum me :  judica judicium meum.
verses.douay_rheims: Res. Thou hast seen, O Lord, their iniquity against me: judge thou my judgment.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra katso, kuinka minulle niin vääryyttä tehdään, ja tuomitse minun oikeuteni.
verses.king_james: O Lord, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidisti omnem furorem,  universas cogitationes eorum adversum me.
verses.douay_rheims: Res. Thou hast seen all their fury, and all their thoughts against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä näet kaikki heidän kostonsa ja kaikki heidän ajatuksensa minusta.
verses.king_james: Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their imaginations against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audisti opprobrium eorum, Domine,  omnes cogitationes eorum adversum me.
verses.douay_rheims: Sin. Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, all their imaginations against me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, sinä kuulet heidän pilkkansa ja kaikki heidän ajatuksensa minua vastaan,
verses.king_james: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Lord, and all their imaginations against me;

verses.latin_vulgate: Labia insurgentium mihi, et meditationes eorum  adversum me tota die.
verses.douay_rheims: Sin. The lips of them that rise up against me: and their devices against me all the day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun vainollisteni huulet ja heidän neuvonsa minua vastaan yli päivää.
verses.king_james: The lips of those that rose up against me, and their device against me all the day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sessionem eorum et resurrectionem eorum vide ;  ego sum psalmus eorum.
verses.douay_rheims: Sin. Behold their sitting down, and their rising up, I am their song.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, kuin he istuvat eli nousevat, niin he minusta virsiä laulavat.
verses.king_james: Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I am their musick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redes eis vicem, Domine,  juxta opera manuum suarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תָּשִׁ֨יב לָהֶ֥ם גְּמ֛וּל יְהֹוָ֖ה כְּמַעֲשֵׂ֥ה יְדֵיהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Thau. Thou shalt render them a recompense, O Lord, according to the works of their hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kosta heille, Herra, niinkuin he ansainneet ovat, heidän kättensä töiden jälkeen.
verses.king_james: Render unto them a recompence, O Lord, according to the work of their hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dabis eis scutum cordis,  laborem tuum.
verses.douay_rheims: Thau. Thou shalt give them a buckler of heart, thy labour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna heidän sydämensä vavista ja sinun kiroustas tuta.
verses.king_james: Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Persequeris in furore, et conteres eos  sub cælis, Domine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּרְדֹּ֤ף בְּאַף֙ וְתַשְׁמִידֵ֔ם מִתַּ֖חַת שְׁמֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Thau. Thou shalt persecute them in anger, and shalt destroy them from under the heavens, O Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vainoo heitä hirmuisuudella, ja hukuta heitä Herran taivaan alta.
verses.king_james: Persecute and destroy them in anger from under the heavens of the Lord.
